perturbation.original_text: 4. COMPENSATION In consideration for the licenses granted hereunder, Company agrees to pay to CSA as follows: a. A [***]in the amount of [***]of Company's revenues from sales of the Property Training Course and all Products after deductions for VAT, returns, refunds b. Company shall be responsible for the tracking of sales of the Property Training Course and all Products containing the Property and providing to CSA: i. A monthly list of the sales of Property Training Course and Product (with supporting sales prices and reconciled reports). ii. Payment to CSA no later than thirty (30) days after receipt of invoice from CSA. CSA shall submit invoice to the Company no later than thirty (30) days after Company provides CSA with the list of the sales of Product (containing the Property with such supporting information that may be required or requested to be disclosed).
perturbation.changed_text: 4. COMPENSATION In consideration for the licenses granted hereunder, Company agrees to pay to CSA as follows: a. A [***]in the amount of [***]of Company's revenues from sales of the Property Training Course and all Products after deductions for VAT, returns, refunds. b. Company shall only be responsible for the tracking of sales of the Property Training Course but not all Products containing the Property and providing to CSA: i. A quarterly list of the sales of Property Training Course and Product (with supporting sales prices and reconciled reports). ii. Payment to CSA no later than sixty (60) days after receipt of invoice from CSA. CSA shall submit invoice to the Company no later than sixty (60) days after Company provides CSA with the list of the sales of Product (containing the Property with such supporting information that may be required or requested to be disclosed).
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in Section 4 by limiting Company's responsibility to tracking sales of only the 'Property Training Course' instead of 'all Products containing the Property', even though 4a mentions all products. This contradicts the obligation to pay royalties on 'all Products' as stated in section 4a and makes it unclear if revenues from all products are subject to royalty. Also the payments and sales reports period changed from monthly to quarterly, and the invoice submission time extended to 60 days, conflicting to the original agreement in the section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. COMPENSATION In consideration for the licenses granted hereunder, Company agrees to pay to CSA as follows: a. A [***]in the amount of [***]of Company's revenues from sales of the Property Training Course and all Products after deductions for VAT, returns, refunds. b. Company shall only be responsible for the tracking of sales of the Property Training Course but not all Products containing the Property and providing to CSA: i. A quarterly list of the sales of Property Training Course and Product (with supporting sales prices and reconciled reports). ii. Payment to CSA no later than sixty (60) days after receipt of invoice from CSA. CSA shall submit invoice to the Company no later than sixty (60) days after Company provides CSA with the list of the sales of Product (containing the Property with such supporting information that may be required or requested to be disclosed). c. CSA shall have the right to request an independent audit of the sales of the Product containing the Property which the Company agrees to assist within a reasonable period of time of such request.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 4 introduces a strong in-text contradiction. Initially, Section 4a states that royalties are based on the revenues from sales of 'all Products'. However, the changed Section 4b limits the company's tracking responsibility to only the 'Property Training Course' and not 'all Products containing the Property.' This directly contradicts Section 4a's obligation to pay royalties on revenues from 'all Products', creating an ambiguity about whether revenues from all products are subject to royalty. Furthermore, the change from monthly to quarterly reports and extending invoice submission from 30 to 60 days creates inconsistencies with the original payment and reporting terms, as described in the original text of Section 4 and now reflected in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: 5. REVIEW OF MATERIALS a. Talent shall have the right of approval in respect of all Product being proposed as any part of the Property appearing or integrated into it and all proposed use of the Property (including to whom the Property is distributed for sale if other than the general public) prior to public release and distribution. b. All Materials or any part thereof shall be sent by the Company to CSA for approval by Talent. c. Talent shall have seven (7) days following receipt of such Materials or any part thereof displaying the intended use of the Property to review and for CSA to provide to Company written approval for such use.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. REVIEW OF MATERIALS a. Talent's approval is only needed for new advertising campaigns but not for all Product being proposed as any part of the Property appearing or integrated into it and all proposed use of the Property (including to whom the Property is distributed for sale if other than the general public) prior to public release and distribution. b. Only New advertising campaigns Materials or any part thereof shall be sent by the Company to CSA for approval by Talent. c. Talent shall have fourteen (14) days following receipt of such Materials or any part thereof displaying the intended use of the Property to review and for CSA to provide to Company written approval for such use.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying section 5, the agreement now states that Talent's approval is only needed for 'new advertising campaigns' instead of all products. This contradicts the initial broad right of approval, creating confusion over which specific uses of the property require talent approval. Also, the reviewing period was extended to 14 days, contradicting with the 7 days originally in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. REVIEW OF MATERIALS a. Talent's approval is only needed for new advertising campaigns but not for all Product being proposed as any part of the Property appearing or integrated into it and all proposed use of the Property (including to whom the Property is distributed for sale if other than the general public) prior to public release and distribution. b. Only New advertising campaigns Materials or any part thereof shall be sent by the Company to CSA for approval by Talent. c. Talent shall have fourteen (14) days following receipt of such Materials or any part thereof displaying the intended use of the Property to review and for CSA to provide to Company written approval for such use. d. In the event that Talent objects to the Products or any part thereof and/or any proposed use of the Property, CSA on behalf of Talent shall submit to Company, within seven (7) days following Company's official submission of materials, a written request for revisions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 5a now limits Talent's approval to only 'new advertising campaigns,' while the original text gave Talent approval rights over 'all Product'. This contradicts the contradicted section, which now includes clause d. referencing a broader approval right that the modified 5a limits. Additionally, the review period was extended from seven to fourteen days, which contradicts the 'within seven (7) days' timeline specified in clause d.

perturbation.original_text: 8. TERMINATION a. Except as provided in this Section 8, this Agreement shall terminate immediately upon the earlier of (i) the Term of this Agreement or (ii) dissolving of Company and/or Company's complete cessation of doing all business. b. Either party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement immediately in the event that Talent or the Company does any of the following: i. Engages in illegal, immoral, or criminal conduct resulting in a felony conviction; ii. Misrepresents or conceals anything in their background that could be detrimental to the value of the endorsement being made; iii. Engages in conduct contrary to the best interests of the other party; iv. Engages in conduct that reasonably offends the sensitivities of a significant portion of the population; or v. Engages in conduct that could bring the other party into public disrepute. c. Either party may terminate this provision at any time during the Term, in the event either party is guilty of a material breach of this Agreement, having been given notice of such breach and the breach not being rectified within a reasonable period of time.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. TERMINATION a. Except as provided in this Section 8, this Agreement shall terminate immediately upon the earlier of (i) the Term of this Agreement or (ii) dissolving of Company and/or Company's complete cessation of doing all business. b. Either party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement within 30 days notice in the event that Talent or the Company does any of the following: i. Engages in illegal, immoral, or criminal conduct resulting in a felony conviction; ii. Misrepresents or conceals anything in their background that could be detrimental to the value of the endorsement being made; iii. Engages in conduct contrary to the best interests of the other party; iv. Engages in conduct that reasonably offends the sensitivities of a significant portion of the population; or v. Engages in conduct that could bring the other party into public disrepute. c. Either party may terminate this provision only at the end of the Term, in the event either party is guilty of a material breach of this Agreement, having been given notice of such breach and the breach not being rectified within a reasonable period of time.
perturbation.explanation: Section 8 is modified to change the termination conditions. Now, termination under section 8b requires '30 days notice' instead of being immediate, and termination due to a material breach (section 8c) can now only occur 'at the end of the Term'. This introduces a contradiction with the clause 8a which mention about immediate terminations. Now the enforceability of immediate terminations vs terminations after 30 days or at the end of the term is debatable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. TERMINATION a. Except as provided in this Section 8, this Agreement shall terminate immediately upon the earlier of (i) the Term of this Agreement or (ii) dissolving of Company and/or Company's complete cessation of doing all business. b. Either party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement within 30 days notice in the event that Talent or the Company does any of the following: i. Engages in illegal, immoral, or criminal conduct resulting in a felony conviction; ii. Misrepresents or conceals anything in their background that could be detrimental to the value of the endorsement being made; iii. Engages in conduct contrary to the best interests of the other party; iv. Engages in conduct that reasonably offends the sensitivities of a significant portion of the population; or v. Engages in conduct that could bring the other party into public disrepute. c. Either party may terminate this provision only at the end of the Term, in the event either party is guilty of a material breach of this Agreement, having been given notice of such breach and the breach not being rectified within a reasonable period of time.
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a contradiction. Initially, section 8 allowed for immediate termination in certain events. The changed text now stipulates a 30-day notice for terminations under section 8b and restricts terminations for material breaches (section 8c) to only occur 'at the end of the Term.' This directly conflicts with the original clause 8a, which states that the agreement shall terminate 'immediately' under certain conditions. The modified text therefore creates a contradiction, as immediate termination and termination after 30 days or at the end of the term cannot both be true simultaneously. The Change breaks compatibility with the contradicted section.
